parsed.summary: The v0.4.6 cumulative diff adds retry logic, BigModel error dispatch, and job tracking, but the core libraries (retry.mjs, glm-client.mjs, tracked-jobs.mjs, bigmodel-errors.mjs) warrant closer inspection for silent-failure and state-corruption risks before shipping. I need to inspect the actual source to ground findings.

findings.title: retry.mjs may silently swallow fatal errors or retry non-retryable failures
findings.body: A new 200+ line retry library is introduced without visible guardrails against retrying non-idempotent operations or exhausting retries silently. If the caller assumes success after retry exhaustion but the retry layer returns a falsy/undefined result, downstream state (job tracking, GLM API submissions) could become inconsistent without any log or error propagation.
findings.recommendation: Inspect retry.mjs to confirm: (1) non-retryable error classes are not retried, (2) final failure always throws or returns a distinguished error object, (3) retry exhaustion is logged, (4) operations retried are confirmed idempotent at the call site.

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs state corruption on partial write or concurrent access
findings.body: Job tracking state persisted to disk is vulnerable to corruption if the process is interrupted mid-write (SIGTERM, OOM, crash). Without atomic writes (write-to-temp + rename), a partially written JSON file would cause all subsequent job queries to fail, potentially losing track of running GLM jobs and making cleanup impossible.
findings.recommendation: Verify that state persistence uses atomic file writes (write to .tmp file then rename). Add JSON parse error recovery that treats corrupted state as empty rather than crashing.

findings.title: bigmodel-errors.mjs error classification may misroute unknown error shapes
findings.body: New BigModel error dispatch maps API error codes to retry/backoff strategies. If the upstream API changes error shapes or returns undocumented codes, the classifier may fall through to a default that either retries infinitely on a permanent error or immediately fails on a transient one. This directly affects the reliability of the auto-retry feature added in v0.4.6.
findings.recommendation: Confirm there is an explicit catch-all/default case that logs the unmapped error shape and applies a conservative strategy (fail fast, or retry once with full backoff). Add a test for unknown error codes.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs backoff/retry integration may duplicate API calls on network timeout
findings.body: When a GLM API call times out at the network layer after the server has already accepted the request, a retry will submit the same job twice. Without an idempotency key or request deduplication, this creates duplicate jobs in the GLM backend that the user is never informed about.
findings.recommendation: Verify that POST-style GLM API calls either (1) include an idempotency key, (2) are not retried on timeout, or (3) the user is warned that retries may create duplicates. Document the tradeoff.

findings.title: configure-gitea-protection.sh executes remote API calls without dry-run or rollback
findings.body: A new shell script configures Gitea branch protection rules via API. If the script fails partway (e.g., after setting some rules but before others), there is no rollback mechanism, leaving the repository in a partially protected state that is hard to detect and diagnose.
findings.recommendation: Add a --dry-run flag and a summary of changes before applying. Consider capturing existing protection state before modification so partial failures can be manually reverted.

findings.title: pre-push hook may block legitimate pushes on stale state
findings.body: The new pre-push hook runs CI checks locally. If the job state file becomes stale (e.g., from a previous interrupted run), the hook may fail with a confusing error, blocking the developer from pushing until they manually clean up state.
findings.recommendation: Ensure pre-push hook failures print a clear remediation message (e.g., 'Run scripts/install-hooks.sh --clean or delete .glm-state/ to reset').

next_steps: Read scripts/lib/retry.mjs in full to verify error propagation and idempotency assumptions
Read scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs to confirm atomic file writes and crash recovery
Read scripts/lib/bigmodel-errors.mjs to verify default/fallthrough behavior for unknown error shapes
Read scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs to check which HTTP methods are retried and whether timeouts are handled specially
Read scripts/glm-companion.mjs to trace the end-to-end retry flow from error detection to user notification
